name: Kiniksa Pharmaceuticals International, plc
business: Kiniksa Pharmaceuticals is a biopharmaceutical company developing and commercializing novel therapies for cardiovascular diseases with unmet need. Its sole marketed product is ARCALYST, an interleukin-1 cytokine trap approved in the United States for recurrent pericarditis, CAPS, and DIRA. The company also has pipeline candidates including KPL-387, KPL-1161, and abiprubart in various stages of development.
sellsTo: patients with recurrent pericarditis in the United States
patients with Cryopyrin-Associated Periodic Syndromes in the United States
patients with Deficiency of Interleukin-1 Receptor Antagonist in the United States

exposures.sentence: ARCALYST is an interleukin-1 (IL-1) and interleukin-1 (IL-1) cytokine trap.
KPL-387 is an investigational, fully human immunoglobulin G2 monoclonal antibody that binds human interleukin-1 receptor 1 (IL-1R1), inhibiting IL-1- and IL-1-mediated signaling.
KPL-1161 is an independently developed, pre-clinical, Fc-modified immunoglobulin G2 monoclonal antibody that binds IL-1R1, inhibiting IL-1- and IL-1-mediated signaling.

We face potential competition from many different sources, including pharmaceutical and biotechnology companies, academic institutions and governmental agencies and public and private research institutions.

catalysts.event: Data from the Phase 2 portion of KPL-387 Phase 2/3 trial in recurrent pericarditis
catalysts.sentence: We expect data from the Phase 2 portion of the trial in the second half of 2026 and plan to use the totality of the data to determine further development strategy.

catalysts.event: Initiation of Phase 1 first-in-human clinical trial for KPL-1161
catalysts.sentence: We are currently conducting preclinical activities with respect to this asset, with an expectation to initiate a Phase 1 first-in-human clinical trial by the end of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 40,000 patients in the United States with recurrent pericarditis seeking medical treatment
tamClaims.sentence: Recurrent pericarditis is a painful inflammatory cardiovascular disease with an estimated United States prevalent population of approximately 40,000 patients seeking and receiving medical treatment.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 14,000 patients in the United States with persistent underlying disease, multiple recurrences, and inadequate response to conventional therapy
tamClaims.sentence: Our commercialization efforts are focused initially on the approximately 14,000 patients in the United States who suffer from persistent underlying disease, multiple recurrences and an inadequate response to conventional therapy.
